properties.name: GetSetupPIN_Requires_Admin_Privilege
properties.claim: GetSetupPIN command SHALL only be invoked by nodes with Administrator privilege.

spec_source.quote: | 0x00 | GetSetupPIN | client → server | GetSetupPINResponse | A F T | M |

violation.description: Non-Admin nodes could invoke GetSetupPIN to obtain Setup PINs for user accounts, enabling unauthorized commissioning or account takeover.
violation.attack_vector: Compromised Operate-level device on fabric attempts to invoke GetSetupPIN to retrieve account credentials
violation.physical_impact: Privacy breach - user account credentials exposed to unauthorized parties

properties.proverifQuery: query invoker:node, tempId:string ⊢ event getsetuppin_invoked(invoker, tempId) ==> has_privilege(invoker, Admin)

properties.name: GetSetupPIN_Requires_Timed_Interaction
properties.claim: GetSetupPIN command SHALL use Timed Interaction to prevent replay attacks.

spec_source.quote: Access: A F T (T indicates Timed Interaction required)

violation.description: Without timed interaction, captured GetSetupPIN commands could be replayed to retrieve Setup PINs at attacker's convenience.
violation.attack_vector: Network-level attacker captures GetSetupPIN request and replays after session timeout

properties.proverifQuery: query cmd:command ⊢ event getsetuppin_executed(cmd) ==> event timed_request_valid(cmd)

properties.name: Login_Requires_Admin_Privilege
properties.claim: Login command SHALL only be invoked by nodes with Administrator privilege.
properties.formal: ∀invoker, tempId, pin. invoke(Login, invoker, tempId, pin) ⟹ has_privilege(invoker, Admin)

spec_source.quote: | 0x02 | Login | client → server | Y | A F T | M |

violation.description: Non-Admin nodes could attempt Login to hijack user sessions or establish unauthorized access to Content Apps.
violation.attack_vector: Compromised device attempts Login with brute-forced PINs to gain account access
violation.physical_impact: Privacy breach - unauthorized access to user content and account

properties.proverifQuery: query invoker:node, tempId:string, pin:string ⊢ event login_invoked(invoker, tempId, pin) ==> has_privilege(invoker, Admin)

properties.name: Login_Requires_Timed_Interaction
properties.claim: Login command SHALL use Timed Interaction to prevent replay attacks.

spec_source.quote: Access: A F T

violation.description: Captured Login commands with valid PINs could be replayed to re-establish sessions.
violation.attack_vector: Replay captured Login command to assume user account

properties.proverifQuery: query cmd:command ⊢ event login_executed(cmd) ==> event timed_request_valid(cmd)

properties.name: Logout_Requires_Operate_Privilege
properties.claim: Logout command SHALL only be invoked by nodes with at least Operate privilege.

spec_source.quote: | 0x03 | Logout | client → server | Y | O F T | M |

violation.description: Nodes without Operate privilege could invoke Logout to disrupt user sessions.
violation.attack_vector: View-only compromised device attempts to log out legitimate users

properties.proverifQuery: query invoker:node ⊢ event logout_invoked(invoker) ==> has_privilege(invoker, Operate)

properties.name: Logout_Requires_Timed_Interaction
properties.claim: Logout command SHALL use Timed Interaction to prevent replay attacks.

spec_source.quote: Access: O F T

violation.description: Captured Logout commands could be replayed to terminate user sessions at attacker's convenience.
violation.attack_vector: Replay Logout command to cause denial of service for user

properties.proverifQuery: query cmd:command ⊢ event logout_executed(cmd) ==> event timed_request_valid(cmd)

properties.name: LoggedOut_Event_Admin_Access
properties.claim: LoggedOut event SHALL only be readable by nodes with Administrator privilege and is marked Sensitive.

spec_source.quote: | 0 | LoggedOut | CRITICAL | A S | O |

violation.description: Non-Admin nodes reading LoggedOut events could learn about user activity patterns and session terminations.
violation.attack_vector: Compromised device monitors LoggedOut events to track user behavior

properties.proverifQuery: query reader:node, nodeId:node_id ⊢ event read_loggedout(reader, nodeId) ==> has_privilege(reader, Admin)

properties.name: Commands_Fabric_Scoped
properties.claim: All Account Login commands SHALL be fabric-scoped, preventing cross-fabric invocation.

spec_source.quote: Access: ... F ... (F indicates Fabric-scoped)

violation.description: Cross-fabric invocation could allow different fabric administrators to manipulate account sessions on other fabrics.
violation.attack_vector: Multi-admin scenario where one fabric attempts to access accounts on another fabric
violation.physical_impact: None directly - unauthorized access across trust domains

properties.proverifQuery: query cmd:command, fabric:fabric_id ⊢ event command_executed(cmd, fabric) ==> authorized_fabric(cmd.invoker, fabric)

properties.name: SetupPIN_Validation_Required
properties.claim: Login command SHALL only succeed if the provided SetupPIN matches the PIN associated with the TempAccountIdentifier.

spec_source.quote: the Content App checks if the account associated with the client's Temp Account Identifier has a current active Setup PIN with the given value

violation.description: Login succeeding without valid PIN would allow unauthorized account access without proper authentication.
violation.attack_vector: Submit Login with arbitrary PIN if validation is bypassed
violation.physical_impact: Privacy breach - full account access without credentials

properties.proverifQuery: query tempId:string, pin:string ⊢ event login_success(tempId, pin) ==> event pin_validated(tempId, pin)

properties.name: SetupPIN_Minimum_Length
properties.claim: SetupPIN SHALL be at least 8 characters in length to provide adequate brute force protection.

spec_source.quote: This field SHALL provide the Setup PIN as a text string at least 8 characters in length.

violation.description: PINs shorter than 8 characters have reduced entropy and are more susceptible to brute force attacks within the rate limit window.
violation.attack_vector: Guess shorter PINs within the 10 attempts per 10 minutes window

properties.proverifQuery: query pin:string ⊢ event pin_accepted(pin) ==> length(pin) >= 8

properties.name: TempAccountIdentifier_Minimum_Length
properties.claim: TempAccountIdentifier SHALL be at least 16 characters to protect against password guessing attacks.

spec_source.quote: The length of this field SHALL be at least 16 characters to protect the account holder against password guessing attacks.

violation.description: Shorter identifiers reduce the guessing space, making it easier for attackers to find valid account identifiers.
violation.attack_vector: Enumerate short TempAccountIdentifiers to find valid accounts

properties.proverifQuery: query tempId:string ⊢ event temp_account_id_accepted(tempId) ==> length(tempId) >= 16

properties.name: Account_Match_Verification
properties.claim: GetSetupPIN SHALL only return a Setup PIN when the account associated with the TempAccountIdentifier matches the active account on the Content App.
properties.formal: ∀tempId, pin. getsetuppin_returns(tempId, pin) ∧ pin ≠ empty ⟹ account_match(tempId, content_app_account)

spec_source.quote: If the accounts are the same, then the Content App returns the GetSetupPIN Response which includes a Setup PIN

violation.description: Returning Setup PIN for non-matching accounts would allow unauthorized commissioning or cross-account access.
violation.attack_vector: Submit TempAccountIdentifier from different user to obtain their Setup PIN

properties.proverifQuery: query tempId:string, pin:string ⊢ event getsetuppin_response(tempId, pin) ∧ pin != '' ==> event account_verified(tempId)

properties.name: GetSetupPIN_Rate_Limiting
properties.claim: GetSetupPIN command SHALL be rate limited to no more than 10 unique requests per 10 minute period to prevent brute force attacks.

spec_source.quote: The server SHALL implement rate limiting to prevent brute force attacks. No more than 10 unique requests in a 10 minute period SHALL be allowed

violation.description: Without rate limiting, attackers could rapidly enumerate TempAccountIdentifiers or brute force Setup PINs.
violation.attack_vector: Send unlimited GetSetupPIN requests to enumerate valid accounts or guess PINs

properties.proverifQuery: query requests:list, interval:time ⊢ count(requests, interval) > 10 ==> event rate_limit_triggered()

properties.name: Login_Rate_Limiting
properties.claim: Login command SHALL be rate limited to no more than 10 unique requests per 10 minute period to prevent brute force attacks.

spec_source.quote: The server SHALL implement rate limiting to prevent brute force attacks. No more than 10 unique requests in a 10 minute period SHALL be allowed

violation.description: Without rate limiting, attackers could brute force Setup PINs for known TempAccountIdentifiers.
violation.attack_vector: Rapid Login attempts with different PIN guesses

properties.proverifQuery: query requests:list, interval:time ⊢ count(requests, interval) > 10 ==> event rate_limit_triggered()

properties.name: TempAccountIdentifier_Max_Validity
properties.claim: TempAccountIdentifier SHALL NOT be valid for more than 10 minutes to limit the attack window.

spec_source.quote: A Content App that supports this command SHALL ensure that the Temporary Account Identifier used by its clients is not valid for more than 10 minutes.

violation.description: Long-lived TempAccountIdentifiers extend the window for brute force attacks and replay attacks.
violation.attack_vector: Collect TempAccountIdentifier and attempt brute force over extended period

properties.proverifQuery: query tempId:string, t_issue:time, t_use:time ⊢ event tempId_used(tempId, t_use) ==> t_use <= t_issue + 600

properties.name: Aggressive_Timeout_Recommendation
properties.claim: Implementations SHOULD impose aggressive timeouts for TempAccountIdentifier to Setup PIN mappings to prevent delayed login.
properties.formal: ∀tempId, pin. mapping(tempId, pin, t) ⟹ timeout(t) < aggressive_threshold

spec_source.quote: Implementations of such a service should impose aggressive time outs for any mapping of Temporary Account Identifier to Setup PIN in order to prevent accidental login due to delayed invocation.

violation.description: Long-lived mappings could allow delayed Login attempts to succeed after the legitimate user's session expectation has ended.
violation.attack_vector: Capture TempAccountIdentifier and PIN, wait for user to leave, then Login

properties.proverifQuery: query tempId:string, pin:string, t:time ⊢ event login_attempt(tempId, pin, t) ==> mapping_still_valid(tempId, pin, t)

properties.name: LoggedOut_Triggers_Access_Removal
properties.claim: When LoggedOut event is received, Fabric Admin SHALL remove access to the Content App for the specified Node.

spec_source.quote: In response to this event, the Fabric Admin SHALL remove access to this Content App by the specified Node.

violation.description: If Fabric Admin fails to remove access, logged-out nodes retain Content App access, violating user's logout intent.
violation.attack_vector: Logged-out node continues accessing Content App if admin fails to revoke access
violation.physical_impact: Privacy breach - continued unauthorized content access

properties.proverifQuery: query nodeId:node_id ⊢ event loggedout_received(nodeId) ==> event access_removed(nodeId)

properties.name: LoggedOut_No_Node_Removes_All_NonAdmin
properties.claim: If LoggedOut event has no Node specified, Fabric Admin SHALL remove access to all non-Admin Nodes.

spec_source.quote: If no Node is provided, then the Fabric Admin SHALL remove access to all non-Admin Nodes.

violation.description: Non-Admin nodes retain access even after global logout, violating user's intent to terminate all sessions.
violation.attack_vector: Previously authorized non-Admin device continues access after user performs global logout
violation.physical_impact: Privacy breach - multiple unauthorized access points remain

properties.proverifQuery: query ⊢ event loggedout_no_node() ==> ∀n. ¬is_admin(n) → event access_removed(n)

properties.name: LoggedOut_Event_Critical_Priority
properties.claim: LoggedOut event SHALL have CRITICAL priority to ensure timely delivery and processing.

spec_source.quote: | 0 | LoggedOut | CRITICAL | A S | O |

violation.description: Lower priority could cause LoggedOut events to be delayed or dropped, leaving stale access grants in place.
violation.attack_vector: Flood system with lower priority events to delay LoggedOut processing

properties.proverifQuery: query event:event ⊢ event_loggedout(event) ==> event.priority == CRITICAL

properties.name: Empty_PIN_Account_Mismatch
properties.claim: GetSetupPINResponse SHALL return empty string when accounts do not match, not null or error.

spec_source.quote: This field SHALL provide the Setup PIN as a text string at least 8 characters in length or empty string to indicate that the accounts do not match.

violation.description: Non-empty response for mismatched accounts could leak credential information or confuse client logic.
violation.attack_vector: Analyze varying responses to determine which TempAccountIdentifiers are valid

properties.proverifQuery: query tempId:string, pin:string ⊢ event getsetuppin_response(tempId, pin) ∧ ¬account_match(tempId) ==> pin == ''

properties.name: Login_Conditional_Activation
properties.claim: Content App MAY make user account active upon successful Login verification (conditional, not guaranteed).

spec_source.quote: If the Setup PIN is valid for the user account associated with the Temp Account Identifier, then the Content App MAY make that user account active.

violation.description: This is a spec gap - MAY language means implementations might not activate accounts even with valid credentials, causing unpredictable behavior.
violation.attack_vector: N/A - implementation variation concern

properties.proverifQuery: N/A - MAY requirement not formally verifiable

properties.name: SetupPIN_String_Format
properties.claim: Setup PIN SHALL be transmitted as a string to accommodate various formats including alpha-numeric encodings.

spec_source.quote: The Setup PIN is a character string so that it can accommodate different future formats, including alpha-numeric encodings.

violation.description: Non-string formats could limit PIN complexity options or cause encoding issues in transit.
violation.attack_vector: N/A - format specification

properties.proverifQuery: query pin:string ⊢ event pin_transmitted(pin) ==> typeof(pin) == string

assumptions.statement: Account verification is performed by a central service outside the scope of the Matter specification.
assumptions.impact_if_violated: If external service is compromised, all authentication properties fail. Matter spec cannot enforce security of external account verification.

assumptions.statement: User is prompted for consent prior to Commissioning before GetSetupPIN is invoked.
assumptions.impact_if_violated: Without user consent, automated brute force or enumeration attacks become more feasible.

assumptions.statement: Admin-level access is legitimate and properly authorized.
assumptions.impact_if_violated: Compromised Admin node can perform all Account Login operations legitimately.

assumptions.statement: Fabric Admin correctly implements LoggedOut event handling.
assumptions.impact_if_violated: Logged-out nodes retain access indefinitely if Fabric Admin fails to revoke.

assumptions.statement: TempAccountIdentifier is derived from Rotating Device Identifier and is unique per session.
assumptions.impact_if_violated: Reused identifiers could enable replay attacks or session confusion.

assumptions.statement: Rate limit counter is not reset by implementation logic during the 10-minute window.
assumptions.source: Derived from Section 6.2.4.1 rate limiting requirement
assumptions.impact_if_violated: Implementation could reset counter, allowing unlimited attempts.

spec_gaps.description: Rate limiting of 10 requests per 10 minutes allows 1,440 attempts per day which may be sufficient for brute-forcing weak PINs.
spec_gaps.security_impact: With 8-character minimum PIN, 10^8 combinations exist. At 1,440/day, full enumeration takes 190 years. However, weak PINs (birthdays, patterns) are vulnerable.

spec_gaps.description: No exponential backoff or permanent lockout after repeated failures specified. Only fixed rate limit exists.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Attacker can sustain low-rate brute force attacks indefinitely without escalating consequences.

spec_gaps.description: No PIN complexity requirements specified beyond minimum length. No requirement for mixed case, numbers, or special characters.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Weak PINs (e.g., '00000000', '12345678') are permitted, reducing effective entropy.

spec_gaps.description: Account verification method is 'outside the scope of this specification'. Security depends entirely on external service implementation.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Matter spec cannot guarantee account security - vulnerabilities in external service propagate to Matter ecosystem.

spec_gaps.description: 'Content App MAY make that user account active' - not SHALL. Successful Login does not guarantee account activation.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Inconsistent implementations could cause user confusion or force users to retry Login, increasing attack surface exposure.

spec_gaps.description: Access removal mechanism and timing not specified. 'Fabric Admin SHALL remove access' but how and when is implementation-dependent.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Delay between LoggedOut event and actual access removal creates window for continued unauthorized access.

spec_gaps.description: No session timeout or automatic logout specified after Login success. Access persistence is undefined.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Once logged in, access may persist indefinitely until explicit Logout, creating long-lived session risk.

spec_gaps.description: 'Implementations... should impose aggressive time outs' uses SHOULD, not SHALL, for TempAccountIdentifier to PIN mapping timeouts.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Long-lived mappings enable delayed Login attacks after legitimate user's session expectation ends.

property.claim: Account Login cluster operates on Content App endpoints which are identified by Application Basic cluster.

cross_cluster_properties.violation_scenario: Account Login on endpoint without Application Basic could enable phantom Content App attacks where login targets undefined applications.

property.claim: Account Login sessions may interact with Content Control parental controls; login should respect content restrictions.

cross_cluster_properties.violation_scenario: Logged-in sessions bypass Content Control restrictions if Content App doesn't enforce parental controls post-login.

safety_critical_properties.failure_mode: Unauthorized access to user's content streaming accounts, viewing history, and personal preferences.
safety_critical_properties.spec_mitigation: PIN validation required, rate limiting, Admin privilege required

safety_critical_properties.failure_mode: Stale access grants allow previously authorized nodes to continue accessing user content after logout.
safety_critical_properties.spec_mitigation: LoggedOut event with CRITICAL priority triggers access removal

safety_critical_properties.failure_mode: Cross-account credential exposure allows unauthorized parties to learn Setup PINs for other users.
safety_critical_properties.spec_mitigation: Account matching required, empty string returned on mismatch
